data.title: Zura Bio slips after CEO announces medical leave
data.description:
• Zura Bio (NASDAQ:ZURA) announced that its Chief Executive Officer, Robert Lisicki, will be on medical leave starting October 10. 
  • As a result, the company's stock has dropped by 3% in premarket trading.
  • The company’s Board has appointed Kim Davis, Chief Operating Officer, to serve as the company’s interim CEO and interim principal executive officer, in addition to her current duties.

data.title: Kalshi's latest financing round pushes its valuation to $5B - report
data.description:
Kalshi plans to raise more than $300M in a new round of financing, giving the online prediction market a valuation of $5B, according to a media report on Friday. 

Riding the popularity of prediction markets — which allow customers to wager on everything from elections to sports to whether Taylor Swift will hold the top 10 spots on the Billboard Hot 100 — Kalshi's valuation will have more than doubled from the $2B valuation it received in June after raising $185M.

In addition to the new round of financing, the company plans to announce that it will start allowing customers in more than 140 countries to place bets on its website, expanding from its current U.S. customer base, the New York Times reported, citing an interview with the company's co-founder and CEO, Tarek Mansour.

The fundraising, disclosed the same week as the New York Stock Exchange's investment of up to $2B in rival Polymarket, highlights just how fast prediction markets have surged in popularity and revenue.

Kalshi is expecting ~$50B in trading volume on an annualized basis, compared with ~$300M annualized volume it logged in 2024.

The company is backed by Sequoia Capital, which first invested in it in 2020, and new investor Andreessen Horowitz. Others participating in this round include Coinbase Ventures (COIN), Paradigm, and CapitalG, the report said.

In March, Robinhood Markets (HOOD) launched a prediction markets hub on its trading platform through a partnership with Kalshi.

data.title: T. Rowe Price sees rise in AUM as of September-end
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Group (NASDAQ:TROW) announced on Friday preliminary September-end assets under management of $1.77T, compared to $1.73T at the end of August.

By asset class, Equity had an AUM of $885B; Fixed income, including money market, of $208B; Multi-asset of $618B; and Alternatives of $56B.

Shares were +0.46% pre-market to $106.50.

Preliminary net outflows were $2.0B for the month of September.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: BMY, QCOM, MOS, LOW
data.description:
Stock index futures inched higher on Friday, a day after Wall Street paused its rally as concerns rose about the length of the U.S. government shutdown. Here are four stocks to watch on the day: 

Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) shares were up 0.5% in premarket trade as it agreed to acquire privately held biotechnology company Orbital Therapeutics for $1.5 billion in cash. The deal strengthens the firm’s cell therapy portfolio. Orbital Therapeutics is advancing a new generation of RNA medicines designed to reprogram cells in vivo, treating diseases at their source. 

Qualcomm (QCOM) was down 1.1% before the opening bell after China's State Administration for Market Regulation reportedly started an investigation into the months-old Qualcomm's (NASDAQ:QCOM) acquisition of Autotalks. The regulator said it would look into potential violations of the country’s anti-monopoly law in relation to Qualcomm's acquisition of connected-vehicle technology provider Autotalks.

Mosaic (MOS) dropped 9.1% in premarket trade after its production and sales volumes for potash in the third quarter were both ~2.3 million tonnes, while phosphate sales came in at 1.6 million tonnes, trailing production due to shipment timing delays. Sales volumes for Mosaic Fertilizantes in the third quarter were approximately 2.8 million tons, a 25% increase from the second quarter.

Lowe's (LOW) stock will be under watch as it could snap its nine straight sessions of losses. The company is currently up 0.4% in premarket trade. The company began its downward trajectory on September 29. As per Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, it is recommended to Hold the stock. However, Wall Street analysts have a Buy call for the stock. The stock has been down 4.9% since the start of the year.

data.title: VolitionRx announces pricing of $6M underwritten public offering of common stock and common stock warrants
data.description:
• VolitionRx (NYSE:VNRX) on Friday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 11.5M shares of its common stock and warrants to purchase up to 11.5M shares of its common stock.
  • The public offering price of for each share of common stock and an accompanying warrant to purchase one share of common stock is $0.52 per set of securities.
  • Each warrant has an exercise price per share of $0.60, is exercisable immediately upon issuance, and expires five years from the issuance date.
  • In connection with the offering, Volition has granted the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.73M shares of its common stock together with warrants to purchase up to 1.73M shares of common stock at the public offering price.
  • All of the securities in the offering are being offered by Volition.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about October 14.
  • Volition intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for research and continued product development, clinical studies, product commercialization, working capital and other general corporate purposes, including potential strategic acquisitions.
  • VNRX -15.6% premarket to $0.506.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Applied Materials in focus as KeyBanc ups price target, but sees China headwinds
data.description:
• Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) was in focus on Friday as KeyBanc Capital Markets upped its price target on the semiconductor equipment firm, though it also expressed China-related caution. 
  • “We are adjusting our estimates to reflect incremental headwinds identified by AMAT via 8-K (~$110M in F4Q and ~$600M in FY26),” KeyBanc analysts wrote in a note to clients. “At the same time, we are increasing our PT to $240 from $220 to reflect our incrementally optimistic view of AMAT’s ability to benefit from, and help drive, the boom in AI-related investment.”
  • The firm now expects Applied Materials to earn $9.37 per share in fiscal 2025, down from a prior view of $9.40, and in-line with the consensus. The firm also cut its estimates for fiscal 2026 and 2027 to $9.67 and $10.91, from $9.90 and $11.06, respectively.
  • Earlier this month, Applied Materials disclosed that a new rule will further hurt its ability to export certain products to China-based customers. The chip equipment maker added that the new ruling is likely to reduce revenue in the fourth-quarter by $110M and roughly $600M in fiscal 2026.

data.title: Orestone announces $2M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Orestone Mining (TSXV:ORS:CA) on Friday announced a non-brokered private placement consisting of up to 25M units at a price of $0.08 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $2M.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each warrant will be exercisable for one common share of the company at a price of $0.16 for one year from the date of issuance.
  • The closing date will be on or about October 30.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eightco launches new pilot program to advance AI authentication for enterprise, stock jumps
data.description:
Eightco (NASDAQ:ORBS) stock was surging as the authentication technology company announced the launch of a new pilot program focused on advancing AI authentication for the enterprise.

Shares were +25.00% Friday pre-market to $10.55.

The initiative aims to develop innovative approaches to address emerging identity and verification challenges.

data.title: Orestone announces $2M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Orestone Mining (TSXV:ORS:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement on Friday consisting of up to 25M units at a price of $0.08 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $2M. 
  • Each unit will consist of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will be exercisable for one common share of the company at a price of $0.16 for one year from the date of issuance.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering i) to further develop its Francisca property located in Salta Province, Argentina; ii) to further develop its Captain property located in the Province of British Columbia, Canada; and iii) for general administrative expenses and working capital purposes.

data.title: Shopify names Jessica Hertz as new COO; chief revenue officer steps down
data.description:
Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) has promoted Jessica Hertz to Chief Operating Officer, effective October 9.

Her promotion follows the departure of Bobby Morrison as the Canadian tech company’s Chief Revenue Officer. Morrison has been with Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) since 2022.

Hertz assumes the COO role left vacant by Kaz Nejatian last month who left for Opendoor. She previously served as Shopify’s (NASDAQ:SHOP) General Counsel, overseeing the company’s global legal, communications, policy, talent, and trust teams. Hertz also served as Biden’s principal deputy counsel while the former U.S. President was Vice President.

“My operating system as COO: make the work visible, shorten feedback loops, deliver in smaller increments, own outcomes end-to-end,” Hertz said in a LinkedIn post. “When the work is visible, ownership is clean, and defaults are sharp, winning is the side effect.”

Her appointment was announced through a  filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shopify (SHOP) shares are fractionally higher into Friday’s open after closing more than 1.5% lower on Thursday.
